description: Graded monofilaments applied to the plantar surface; withdrawal threshold is the 50% response filament.
Increasing pressure applied to paw with an analgesiometer; threshold is force at withdrawal/vocalization.
Noxious punctate stimulus; response duration or frequency is scored.
Radiant heat focused on plantar surface; withdrawal latency measured.
Animal placed on 50–55°C plate; latency to licking/jumping recorded.
Cold or evaporative stimulus; withdrawal duration scored.
Radiant heat on tail; time to tail withdrawal recorded.
Facial action-unit scoring (orbital tightening, nose bulge, ear/whisker change).
Analgesic-paired chamber preference indicates tonic pain relief.
Balloon distension of distal colon; visceromotor response (EMG) recorded.
Intraperitoneal acetic acid induces writhing; count over 15–30 min.
